title: Analysis campaign summary: bottleneck-accuracy audit
summary: A single evidence slice audited the experiment metrics, calibration parameters, and NCU bottleneck labels. The launch-overhead correction was the dominant improvement. The remaining error is concentrated in white-box-to-NCU bottleneck mapping: the model frequently predicts memory-bound behavior while NCU reports compute-bound.

sections.slice_run: Audit experiment metrics, calibration gains, and NCU bottleneck mapping.
sections.stable_support: The improved predictor reduced validation MAPE from 55.64% to 12.62% (Δ +43.02 pp) and query MAPE from 43.12% to 10.01% (Δ +33.12 pp). Validation within-30% improved to 93.3% and query within-30% to 96.4%. Launch-overhead correction (launch_fixed_us=60.0) was the dominant fix.
sections.partial_support: NCU bottleneck accuracy is 60.0% on validation (10 profiled) and 74.3% on query (35 profiled), just below the 75% target.
sections.contradiction: All NCU-profiled configs are labeled compute-bound, but the white-box model predicts memory-bound (hbm/tma) for a substantial share of them.
sections.unresolved_ambiguity: The validation profiled subset is only 10 configs, so 60% may be noisy. It is unclear whether the gap is best fixed by raising compute-component costs or by a coarser compute/memory binary mapping.

key_findings.finding_1: MAPE and within-30% thresholds are satisfied; bottleneck accuracy is the only failing gate.
key_findings.finding_2: The bottleneck miss is systematic: predicted memory-bound vs NCU compute-bound.
key_findings.finding_3: Remaining error sources include coarse white-box→NCU bottleneck mapping and unmodeled scheduler/warp effects.
